titleStmt.title: The Acting United States Representative at the United Nations ( Johnson ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Acting United States Representative at the United Nations ( Johnson ) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
New York , August 26, 1946—6:15 p.m.
[Received August 26—5:30 p.m.]

div.p: 520. Reference my 517, August 26, 2:30 p.m. Lie told me today that both Gromyko and Sobolev had been much impressed by the extent and character of our proposals regarding membership as outlined orally and in our draft statement at the meeting reported in my 515, August 23.

Lie indicated his belief that Sobolev who is now in Paris will endeavor to persuade the Soviet Foreign Minister not to oppose our proposal for admission of all 9 members. Gromyko has told Lie that he has definite instructions to veto the applications of Portugal, Ireland and Trans-Jordan. Lie now believes that both Gromyko and Sobolev will use their influence for whatever it may be worth to secure modification of the instructions. Lie stated that he has talked personally with each member of the Security Council and urged that our proposal be accepted.
